title: The Secret Code

jokes.text: They got us trained to use the cash machine. We're chickens in an experiment...waiting for that pellet to come down the shoot.
jokes.setup: Jerry observing people at ATM machines
jokes.punchline: Comparing humans to lab rats/chickens waiting for food pellets
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor with a clever behavioral psychology twist. Well-structured metaphor that rings true.

jokes.text: The here-comes-the-money sound, you know? It's exciting. Don't you get excited? 'It's coming. It's coming. They're giving me money.'
jokes.setup: Building on the ATM training concept
jokes.punchline: Mimicking the childlike excitement of getting money from a machine
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the opening bit, captures the absurd psychology of ATM excitement

jokes.text: So you're taking relationship advice from Chemical Bank now?
jokes.setup: George citing bank security warnings to avoid sharing his ATM code
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the absurdity of using bank policy as relationship guidance
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing. Sharp observation about misplaced priorities in relationships.

jokes.text: Why does everything have to be 'us'? Is there no 'me' left? Why can't there be some things just for me? Is that so selfish?
jokes.setup: George's philosophical defense of keeping his ATM code secret
jokes.punchline: His oblivious question about whether wanting privacy is selfish
jokes.explanation: Pure George - self-centered rationalization delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Actually, that's the definition of selfish.
jokes.setup: George's oblivious question about selfishness
jokes.punchline: Jerry's matter-of-fact correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response. Maximum impact with minimal words.

jokes.text: Have you given your code to anyone? No one's ever asked. Do you want it? It's Jor-EI. Superman's father on Krypton. Of course.
jokes.setup: Question about sharing ATM codes
jokes.punchline: Jerry immediately offers his code and it's Superman-related, which George finds predictable
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jerry's openness vs George's secrecy, plus his predictable Superman obsession

jokes.text: What if my life depended on it? If you're in some situation where fast cash will save your life...l'll give you the code.
jokes.setup: Jerry testing George's resolve about the code
jokes.punchline: George's oddly specific exception for life-threatening cash emergencies
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency - George finding absurd loopholes to maintain his position

jokes.text: My foot fell asleep. How did your foot fall asleep? I crossed my legs. I forgot to alternate.
jokes.setup: Kramer explaining his sleeping foot
jokes.punchline: The oddly technical explanation about forgetting to alternate leg crossing
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer - treating a mundane thing with scientific precision

jokes.text: You're lucky. At least you got something to do.
jokes.setup: Fred hearing about Kramer's sleeping foot
jokes.punchline: Envying someone whose foot fell asleep
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny take on boredom - finding excitement in minor bodily malfunctions

jokes.text: Did you hear that? He said, 'Nice to meet you.' So? So we've met before. At Katie Ash's party. We talked for, like, 10 minutes.
jokes.setup: Fred not remembering meeting Elaine
jokes.punchline: Elaine's indignation at being forgettable
jokes.explanation: Sets up Elaine's subplot nicely, relatable social awkwardness

jokes.text: I talked about how my uncle worked...in the book depository building with Lee Harvey Oswald. Not ringing a bell. When my uncle said to him, 'The president's been shot'...Oswald winked at him and said, 'I'm gonna go catch a movie.'
jokes.setup: Elaine trying to remind Fred of their memorable conversation
jokes.punchline: The absurdly dramatic JFK assassination story that should be unforgettable
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly escalated - what could be more memorable than a JFK conspiracy story? Dark humor with historical edge.

jokes.text: I remember someone had played tic-tac-toe on it and the X's won. They went diagonally from the top left to the bottom right.
jokes.setup: Fred supposedly not remembering Elaine or their conversation
jokes.punchline: He remembers incredibly specific details about bathroom door graffiti
jokes.explanation: Perfect comic irony - remembering trivial bathroom details while forgetting a person completely

jokes.text: I'm gonna do TV spots for Leapin' Larry's Appliance Store. That was Leapin' Larry himself.
jokes.setup: Kramer got a commercial gig
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous name 'Leapin' Larry's'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Leapin' Larry storyline, mildly amusing business name

jokes.text: I'm watching the watchers, Jerry.
jokes.setup: Kramer showing off his emergency scanner
jokes.punchline: The pseudo-philosophical 'watching the watchers' line
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer pretentiousness - making surveillance sound philosophical

jokes.text: Yeah, those civil servants who risk their lives really got it made.
jokes.setup: Kramer saying he'd give everything up to be a fireman
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sarcastic response about dangerous public service jobs being cushy
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic deflation of Kramer's romanticizing

jokes.text: You're lucky they let you drive a car.
jokes.setup: Kramer talking about wanting to steer the back of a fire truck
jokes.punchline: Jerry's cutting assessment of Kramer's driving abilities
jokes.explanation: Sharp putdown that plays on established Kramer characteristics

jokes.text: They're taking the West Side Highway. At this time of day? That's insane. They're heading straight into gridlock. Oh, those fools.
jokes.setup: Kramer listening to emergency scanner
jokes.punchline: His backseat driving of emergency responders via radio
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer - thinking he knows better than trained professionals about their own job

jokes.text: Really? I'm surprised. He doesn't meet that many women.
jokes.setup: Elaine complaining that Fred doesn't remember her
jokes.punchline: Jerry's backhanded insult suggesting Fred should remember since he has so few options
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry - indirect insult disguised as logical observation

jokes.text: I don't know anything about your cycles.
jokes.setup: Susan saying they shouldn't keep secrets
jokes.punchline: George's awkward reference to menstrual cycles
jokes.explanation: Typical George awkwardness, but relies on easy biological humor

jokes.text: We're out of Bosco.
jokes.setup: Domestic conversation about secrets
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of Bosco (later revealed as George's ATM code)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup that seems throwaway but becomes crucial. High craft for structure but low immediate impact.

jokes.text: When I lost my leg in the boating accident last year...I got so depressed about this damn prosthetic...I thought I was gonna have to give up the business. But now, I'm rejuvenated.
jokes.setup: Larry showing Kramer around, mentioning renovation problems
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing he has a prosthetic leg while still calling himself 'Leapin' Larry'
jokes.explanation: Dark irony - the absurdity of keeping the 'Leapin'' name after losing a leg

jokes.text: [Kramer's foot falls asleep and he mimics Larry's limp]
jokes.setup: Kramer's foot falling asleep during Larry's story
jokes.punchline: Visual comedy of Kramer accidentally mimicking the disabled Larry
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy callback that creates maximum cringe - unintentionally mocking disability

jokes.text: That is a great impression.
jokes.setup: Larry seeing Kramer limp
jokes.punchline: Larry thinking Kramer is doing it on purpose and complimenting him
jokes.explanation: Maximizes the cringe - Larry's positive reaction to what he thinks is mockery

jokes.text: You'd think he'd have a sense of humour.
jokes.setup: Larry getting offended at what he thinks was intentional mockery
jokes.punchline: Kramer thinking Larry should laugh at disability jokes about himself
jokes.explanation: Kramer's oblivious expectation that people should laugh at their own disabilities

jokes.text: You just joked yourself out of that commercial, didn't you, munjamba?
jokes.setup: Kramer losing the commercial due to the misunderstanding
jokes.punchline: Jerry's made-up word 'munjamba' as an insult
jokes.explanation: Seinfeld neologism - Jerry creating nonsense insults on the fly

jokes.text: The amazing thing is you never have any place to go.
jokes.setup: Kramer boasting about his navigation skills
jokes.punchline: Jerry pointing out Kramer has no destinations to navigate to
jokes.explanation: Perfect character observation - deflating Kramer's pride with simple logic

jokes.text: I don't like being on, Jerry. I'd much rather be off. Trust me, you're off.
jokes.setup: George worried about having to be 'on' for dinner
jokes.punchline: Jerry's double meaning - George is definitely 'off' (weird)
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay setup and payoff with perfect Jerry delivery

jokes.text: Once the button falls off, that's it. I'll never fix it.
jokes.setup: Fred explaining why he's depressed about his shirt
jokes.punchline: His definitive acceptance of being unable to perform basic repairs
jokes.explanation: Relatable male incompetence humor, establishes Fred as pathetic

jokes.text: None that I can...remember.
jokes.setup: Fred asking if Elaine has other plans for dinner
jokes.punchline: Elaine's pointed emphasis on 'remember' referencing his forgetfulness
jokes.explanation: Subtle callback that shows Elaine's passive-aggressive side

jokes.text: All right. You're locked up in a prison in Turkey. I have your wallet. The only way I can bribe the guards to get you out...is for you to give me your ATM code. Call the embassy. They're closed. Why? Bomb threat.
jokes.setup: Jerry creating an elaborate scenario where George needs to share his code
jokes.punchline: The increasingly ridiculous complications (embassy closed due to bomb threat)
jokes.explanation: Great escalating absurdity as Jerry creates increasingly specific obstacles

jokes.text: J. Peterman. J. Crew.
jokes.setup: Formal introduction between Peterman and George
jokes.punchline: George's confused response mixing up J. Peterman with J. Crew
jokes.explanation: Simple confusion joke that establishes George's social awkwardness

jokes.text: But not to worry. I'll tell the maître d' it'll just be the three bulls.
jokes.setup: Elaine canceling dinner
jokes.punchline: Peterman's pretentious way of saying 'three guys'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Peterman pretentiousness - making dining sound like a nature documentary

jokes.text: Come on, weave your web, liar-man. I got nothing. l... I'm blank. Come on, George...what's the matter with you? I'm choking.
jokes.setup: George needing to come up with an excuse to escape dinner
jokes.punchline: His complete inability to lie under pressure
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - George's usual scheming abilities deserting him when needed most

jokes.text: Well, George...we dine.
jokes.setup: Jerry escaping, leaving George trapped with Peterman
jokes.punchline: Peterman's formal, ominous announcement of their dining
jokes.explanation: Perfect Peterman delivery - making dinner sound like a formal ceremony

jokes.text: And there, tucked into the river's bend, was the object of my search...the Guangia River Market. Fabrics and spices traded under a starlit sky. It was there that I discovered the Pamplona Beret. Sizes seven and a half through eight and three quarters. Price, $35.
jokes.setup: Peterman telling an elaborate travel story
jokes.punchline: The romantic narrative ending with mundane retail details
jokes.explanation: Perfect Peterman - romantic storytelling punctured by commercial reality

jokes.text: You know, this is very nice, but I really could take a cab, really... Nonsense, George. Besides, it gives me a chance to tell you about my trip to Burma. I discovered a very unusual corduroy.
jokes.setup: George trying to escape Peterman's stories
jokes.punchline: Peterman using the car ride as an excuse for more elaborate tales
jokes.explanation: George's mounting horror at being trapped with Peterman's storytelling

jokes.text: It's my mother. She's at death's door. What? I just pray to God...we can make it there in time.
jokes.setup: Peterman getting a phone call
jokes.punchline: Dramatic shift from catalog stories to family emergency
jokes.explanation: Sudden tonal shift that traps George in an even more awkward situation

jokes.text: You're attracted to him because he can't remember you. I am? But that's so sick. That's God's plan. He doesn't really want anyone to get together.
jokes.setup: Jerry analyzing Elaine's attraction to forgetful Fred
jokes.punchline: Jerry's cynical theory about divine relationship sabotage
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld philosophy - finding cosmic meaning in dating neuroses

jokes.text: I'm here for you, Mama.
jokes.setup: Peterman at his dying mother's bedside
jokes.punchline: His theatrical, over-dramatic delivery in a serious moment
jokes.explanation: Peterman can't turn off his dramatic persona even in genuine crisis

jokes.text: I'm George Costanza. I was having dinner with your son.
jokes.setup: George introducing himself to a dying woman
jokes.punchline: The awkwardness of explaining his random presence at a deathbed
jokes.explanation: Peak awkwardness - George's inability to handle serious situations gracefully

jokes.text: The funny thing is, you know, I would really love to tell someone...because it's killing me.
jokes.setup: George talking to the dying woman about his secret code
jokes.punchline: His poor word choice saying the secret is 'killing' him to a dying person
jokes.explanation: Dark irony in George's thoughtless word choice in front of dying woman

jokes.text: It's Bosco. You know, the chocolate syrup. I love that stuff. I pour it in milk. It's my favourite drink.
jokes.setup: George finally revealing his secret ATM code
jokes.punchline: The childish simplicity of 'Bosco' and his innocent explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character revelation - George's code being something so innocuous and childlike

jokes.text: Bosco. Mama? Quiet, quiet. It's a secret. Bosco! Bosco! Shut up. It's a secret. Mama, what are you trying to say? Bosco. She's gone.
jokes.setup: The dying woman hearing George's secret and trying to repeat it
jokes.punchline: Her final word being 'Bosco' and George frantically trying to keep it secret
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - a woman's dying word being an ATM code. Perfect escalation and timing.

jokes.text: I reached down and there was nothing there.
jokes.setup: George explaining his inability to come up with an excuse earlier
jokes.punchline: His dramatic description of mental blankness
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor for creative bankruptcy, very George-like dramatization

jokes.text: Well, we can throw out birthdays immediately. That's too obvious. And no numbers for you. You're a word man. All right. Let's go deeper. What kind of man are you? Well, you're weak, spineless. A man of temptations.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to guess George's ATM code
jokes.punchline: His brutally accurate psychological profiling of George
jokes.explanation: Masterful character analysis disguised as code-guessing. Jerry's psychological insight is brutal and accurate.

jokes.text: Oh, you may stray...but you'll always return to your dark master...the cocoa bean. And only the purest syrup nectar...can satisfy you. If you could, you'd guzzle it by the gallon.
jokes.setup: Jerry's psychological profiling continuing
jokes.punchline: Treating chocolate addiction like drug dependency with overwrought language
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - treating candy like heroin with pseudo-poetic language

jokes.text: Shut up! Shut up!
jokes.setup: Jerry getting uncomfortably close to the truth about chocolate
jokes.punchline: George's panicked reaction to being psychologically exposed
jokes.explanation: Perfect character reaction - George can't handle being seen so clearly

jokes.text: When Mama said 'Bosco'...she must have been communicating something. Her legacy. A dying wish, perhaps.
jokes.setup: Peterman trying to find meaning in his mother's last word
jokes.punchline: The dramatic interpretation of what was actually just an overheard ATM code
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Peterman finding deep meaning in something completely mundane

jokes.text: I'm speaking at a woman's rights conference. Yes, and I'm speaking at a men's conference.
jokes.setup: Elaine making up an excuse to leave
jokes.punchline: George's follow-up excuse that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: George's reflexive lying leading to nonsensical excuses

jokes.text: Perhaps Bosco was this man's name.
jokes.setup: Peterman continuing to analyze his mother's last word
jokes.punchline: Now theorizing Bosco was his mother's secret lover
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - now George's chocolate syrup code becomes a family scandal

jokes.text: You know...the important thing is that you learned something. No, I didn't.
jokes.setup: Jerry suggesting Kramer learned from his commercial disaster
jokes.punchline: Kramer's honest admission of learning nothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer - completely honest about his inability to grow or change

jokes.text: The fact is, we feel things are fine the way they are.
jokes.setup: Kramer trying to help the fire department with his shortcuts
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic resistance to any improvement
jokes.explanation: Standard bureaucracy joke - institutional resistance to change

jokes.text: Attention, company 390. Structure fire...at Leapin' Larry's Appliance Warehouse. Leapin' Larry's? Hey, that's uptown. You gotta take Amsterdam. Stay out of this, Kramer.
jokes.setup: Fire call coming in for Larry's store
jokes.punchline: Kramer immediately trying to give directions despite being rejected
jokes.explanation: Good payoff - Kramer's navigation expertise becomes immediately relevant

jokes.text: What am I doing? I'm on a date with this guy because he didn't remember me? He's demented. Listen to him.
jokes.setup: Elaine realizing the absurdity of her situation with Fred
jokes.punchline: Her moment of clarity about her own neurotic behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character growth moment - Elaine recognizing her own dysfunction

jokes.text: Desota's down, but Cosmo's got the caboose.
jokes.setup: Kramer somehow ending up driving the back of the fire truck
jokes.punchline: His overly confident radio announcement using his real name
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff for Kramer's earlier fire truck dreams

jokes.text: The machine won't open without the code. George, there's no time. Tell him your code. Shout out your code, man. Code! The code!
jokes.setup: Larry trapped by ATM machine during fire
jokes.punchline: The ultimate scenario where George's code secrecy becomes life-threatening
jokes.explanation: Perfect plot convergence - the exact scenario George said he'd make an exception for

jokes.text: Anyone for Bosco?
jokes.setup: Everyone now knowing George's secret code
jokes.punchline: George casually offering the chocolate syrup his code was named after
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback line that ties together the entire episode's themes

jokes.text: 'The Rogue's Wallet. It's where he kept his card, his dirty little secret. Short, devious, balding...his name was Costanza. He killed my mother.'
jokes.setup: Reading from new J. Peterman catalog
jokes.punchline: Peterman turning George into a dramatic catalog character who 'killed' his mother
jokes.explanation: Brilliant finale that transforms the episode's events into Peterman's dramatic catalog copy. Perfect character integration and callback structure.

tmdb_overview: George dines with Elaine's boss; Jerry's sleeping foot costs him a commercial; Kramer gives traffic advice to firemen.
